segments.text: MELANIE:Excuse me, DR JOHNSON.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：您好，约翰逊博士。

segments.text: May I speak to you for a minute?
segments.translation: 我能跟您聊一会吗？

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Sure.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：当然。

segments.text: Come in.
segments.translation: 请进。

segments.text: MELANIE:I'm MELANIE Griffin.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：我是梅兰妮·格里芬。

segments.text: I'm taking your course in Population Studies.
segments.translation: 我是您人口研究课的学生。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Right.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：好的。

segments.text: Well, MELANIE, how can I help you?
segments.translation: 梅兰妮，那我能帮你什么呢？

segments.text: MELANIE:I'm...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：我..

segments.text: having a bit of trouble with the second assignment, and it's due in twelve days.
segments.translation: 第二个作业我遇到点问题，还有12天就要交了

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:What sort of trouble are you having?
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：什么问题呢？

segments.text: Is the assignment question a problem?
segments.translation: 是作业题目吗？

segments.text: MELANIE:Well, that's part of the problem.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：嗯，其中一部分吧。

segments.text: I'm also having - been having - trouble getting hold of the books.
segments.translation: 我有，其实一直有个问题是找不到需要的书。

segments.text: I've been to the library several times, and all the books are out.
segments.translation: 我已经去过几次图书馆了，所有书都借出了。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Sounds like you should have started borrowing books a bit earlier.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：听起来你该早点开始借书啊。

segments.text: MELANIE:Well, I had  a really big assignment due in for another course, and I've been spending all my time on that, and I thought...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：嗯，其实是我另外一门课有个大作业要交，我一直把时间都花在那上面，想着......

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:...You might get an extension of time to finish your assignment for me?
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：在我这，你可以延迟交作业的日期。

segments.text: MELANIE:If that's possible, but I don't know ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：可能吗？但是，我不知道...

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Well, yes, it is possible, but extensions are normally given only for  medical or compassionate reasons, otherwise it's really a question of organising your study, and we don't like giving extensions to students who simply didn't plan their work properly.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：嗯是可能的，不过一般延迟日期只批给医疗和私事原因。否则这就是你组织学习的问题了，我们也不愿意让一些不会合理安排学习时间的学生延迟交作业。

segments.text: What did you get for your first assignment?
segments.translation: 那你上次的作业分数是多少？

segments.text: MELANIE:I got eighty-seven percent.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：我拿了78分。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Mmm, yes, you did very well indeed, so obviously you can produce good work.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：嗯，成绩很好啊。很明显你的作业质量很高。

segments.text: MELANIE:I don't think I'll need too much extra time, as long as I can get hold of some of the important references.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：我不需要延迟太久，只要我拿到一些重要的参考资料。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Well, since you did so well in your first assignment.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：鉴于你上次作业做得那么好，

segments.text: I'm prepared to give you an extra two weeks for this one, so that'll mean you'll need to submit it about a month from now.
segments.translation: 我就批你延迟2个星期交吧，也就是你得下个月交。

segments.text: MELANIE:Thank you.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：太感谢了。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Now, what about the reading materials?
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：那你的阅读资料到底怎么了？

segments.text: Have you checked out the journal articles in the list?
segments.translation: 你查阅过单子上的期刊文章吗？

segments.text: MELANIE:Umm, no, not yet, there were about twenty of them, and I wasn't sure which ones would be most useful or important.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：额，还没呢。有20个，我不是很清楚到底哪些最有用和最重要。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Well, they're all useful, but I don't expect anyone to read them all, because a number of them deal with the same issues.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：好吧，都很有用，但是我也不指望你们都读了，毕竟里面很多讲得是同一个问题。

segments.text: Let me give you some suggestions.
segments.translation: 让我给你提些建议吧。

segments.text: The article by Anderson and Hawker is really worth reading.
segments.translation: 安德森和霍克写的文章很值得一读。

segments.text: MELANIE:Right.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的。

segments.text: I'll read that one.
segments.translation: 我会看那个的。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:You should also read the article by Jackson, but just look at the part on the  research methodology - how they did it.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：你还该看杰克逊写的那篇文章，但是只需看研究方法论，就是他们如何做的。

segments.text: MELANIE:OK ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的…

segments.text: Jackson, got that...
segments.translation: 杰克逊，知道了…

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:And if you have time, the one by Roberts says very relevant things, although it's not essential.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：如果时间充裕的话，可以看看罗伯茨的文章，有相关的资料，但是不是必须的。

segments.text: MELANIE:So, OK, if it's  useful, I'll try and read that one ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：那......如果有用的话我会读读看。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Now, the one by Morris.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：还有莫里斯的，

segments.text: I wouldn't bother with that at this stage, if I were you.
segments.translation: 我是你的话，现在阶段来说可以不要理会。

segments.text: MELANIE:OK, I  won't bother with Morris.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的，忽略莫里斯的。

segments.text: Oh, now, someone told me the article by Cooper is important.
segments.translation: 噢，还有人告诉我库伯的文章也很重要。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Well, yes, in a way, but just look at  the last part, where he discusses the research results.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：嗯，某程度来说是。但是只要看最后讨论研究结果那一部分就好。

segments.text: And lastly, there's Forster - I can't think why I included that one.
segments.translation: 最后是福斯特。我都不知道为什么我要加上他的。

segments.text: It's not bad and could be of some help, but not that much.
segments.translation: 不错，有点用但是又不是很重要。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Now, let's deal with the assignment question.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：现在我们来看看作业的问题。

segments.text: What's the problem there?
segments.translation: 有什么问题啊？

segments.text: MELANIE:It's the graph on page two.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：这是第二页的图。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:What seems to be the problem?
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：有什么问题呢？

segments.text: It's just the bar graph showing reasons why people change where they live.
segments.translation: 就是一副描述人们迁移住所原因的柱状图。

segments.text: MELANIE:Well.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好。

segments.text: I've got a photocopy but the reasons at the bottom are missing.
segments.translation: 我有复印件，但是底下的原因没印上。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Ah, OK.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：噢，这样！

segments.text: Look at the first bar on the graph - now that indicates the number of people who move because they want more space.
segments.translation: 先看看图中第一个柱形，说得是因为需要更多空间而搬的人数。

segments.text: MELANIE:Oh I see ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：哦，我明白了…

segments.text: bar one.
segments.translation: 第一条。

segments.text: OK ...
segments.translation: 好…

segments.text: Now what about the next bar?
segments.translation: 那第二个柱形呢？

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Bar two is to do with  the people living nearby disturbing them, so they chose to move away to somewhere quieter.
segments.translation: 第二个柱形说的是因为邻居扰民，为了搬去安静点的地方的人数。

segments.text: Now let's look at bar number three ...
segments.translation: 现在来看看第三个柱形，

segments.text: another reason people change their place of living is because they want to be closer to the city.
segments.translation: 说的是因为想住的近市区而搬的人数。

segments.text: MELANIE:OK.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的。

segments.text: Proximity to the city is an issue...
segments.translation: 靠近市区是个问题。

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Now ...
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：现在…

segments.text: bar number four refers to problems when the owner of the property won't help fix things that go wrong.
segments.translation: 第四个柱形是因为房东不肯负责维修而搬迁的人数。

segments.text: In other words, the owner is not helpful and so the tenants move out.
segments.translation: 换句话说就是房东不给力，租客搬离。

segments.text: MELANIE:OK ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的…

segments.text: Now what about bar five?
segments.translation: 那第五个呢？

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Bar five is about those people who move because they need a bus or train to get them into the city or to go to work.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：第五个柱形图指的是因为需要坐巴士或火车到市区上班的人数。

segments.text: MELANIE:OK ...
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：好的…

segments.text: And bar six?
segments.translation: 那第六个呢？

segments.text: DR JOHNSON:Bar number six is interesting.
segments.translation: 约翰逊博士：第六个原因挺有意思，

segments.text: That reason was given quite a lot - people moving because they wanted to be in a more attractive neighbourhood.
segments.translation: 包含意思也很广，说的是，搬是因为想住在更好的小区。

segments.text: MELANIE:Oh, yes, thank you very much.
segments.translation: 梅兰妮：嗯，太谢谢您了。
